title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot meets with Spence on a glass-enclosed terrace in Woodleigh Village. Spence explains that he moved in with his sister, Elsa, after her husband passed away and her children moved abroad. While sharing spiced ginger beer, the two men discuss a recent murder case involving a child whose head was found in a water bucket. 

Poirot attempts to enlist Spence's help, arguing that despite Spence's long retirement from the police, he still possesses a detective's perspective and access to rumors. Spence expresses skepticism, noting that modern social dynamics and the tendency of women to defend "bad boy" partners make investigations difficult. 

The conversation shifts to their shared history. Poirot reveals he was asked to investigate the current case by the mystery novelist Ariadne Oliver. This prompts a recollection of a past case involving the murder of Miss Macquet, which Spence had previously asked Poirot to solve. They briefly reminisce about a difficult individual from that old case, described as a man suited to being a mortician. 

Poirot clarifies that Mrs. Oliver was staying at the house of a woman named Mrs. Butler, a widow whose husband was a pilot. Poirot explains that Mrs. Oliver traveled to London to find him because she was deeply uneasy about the incident and wanted him to intervene. Although Spence acknowledges that he also sought Poirot's help, he insists that he cannot assist with the current investigation.
